NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. Set to “Do Not Destroy.” A Michtam of David when he fled from Saul into the cave. Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful to me! For my soul trusts in You; And in the shadow of Your wings I will make my refuge, Until these calamities have passed by.
NKJV.2: I will cry out to God Most High, To God who performs all things for me.
NKJV.3: He shall send from heaven and save me; He reproaches the one who would swallow me up. Selah God shall send forth His mercy and His truth.
NKJV.4: My soul is among lions; I lie among the sons of men Who are set on fire, Whose teeth are spears and arrows, And their tongue a sharp sword.
NKJV.5: Be exalted, O God, above the heavens; Let Your glory be above all the earth.
NKJV.6: They have prepared a net for my steps; My soul is bowed down; They have dug a pit before me; Into the midst of it they themselves have fallen. Selah
NKJV.7: My heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast; I will sing and give praise.
NKJV.8: Awake, my glory! Awake, lute and harp! I will awaken the dawn.
NKJV.9: I will praise You, O Lord, among the peoples; I will sing to You among the nations.
NKJV.10: For Your mercy reaches unto the heavens, And Your truth unto the clouds.
NKJV.11: Be exalted, O God, above the heavens; Let Your glory be above all the earth.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, Altaschith<S>516</S>, Michtam<S>4387</S> of David<S>1732</S>, when he fled<S>1272</S> from<S>6440</S> Saul<S>7586</S> in the cave<S>4631</S>. KJV.3: He shall send<S>7971</S> from heaven<S>8064</S>, and save<S>3467</S> me from the reproach<S>2778</S> of him that would swallow me up<S>7602</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. God<S>430</S> shall send forth<S>7971</S> his mercy<S>2617</S> and his truth<S>571</S>. <sup>from the: or, he reproacheth him that</sup>
KJV.4: My soul<S>5315</S> is among<S>8432</S> lions<S>3833</S>: and I lie<S>7901</S> even among them that are set on fire<S>3857</S>, even the sons<S>1121</S> of men<S>120</S>, whose teeth<S>8127</S> are spears<S>2595</S> and arrows<S>2671</S>, and their tongue<S>3956</S> a sharp<S>2299</S> sword<S>2719</S>.
KJV.5: Be thou exalted<S>7311</S>, O God<S>430</S>, above the heavens<S>8064</S>; let thy glory<S>3519</S> be above all the earth<S>776</S>.
KJV.6: They have prepared<S>3559</S> a net<S>7568</S> for my steps<S>6471</S>; my soul<S>5315</S> is bowed down<S>3721</S>: they have digged<S>3738</S> a pit<S>7882</S> before<S>6440</S> me, into the midst<S>8432</S> whereof they are fallen<S>5307</S> themselves. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.7: My heart<S>3820</S> is fixed<S>3559</S>, O God<S>430</S>, my heart<S>3820</S> is fixed<S>3559</S>: I will sing<S>7891</S> and give praise<S>2167</S>. <sup>fixed: or, prepared</sup>
KJV.8: Awake up<S>5782</S>, my glory<S>3519</S>; awake<S>5782</S>, psaltery<S>5035</S> and harp<S>3658</S>: I myself will awake<S>5782</S> early<S>7837</S>.
KJV.9: I will praise<S>3034</S> thee, O Lord<S>136</S>, among the people<S>5971</S>: I will sing<S>2167</S> unto thee among the nations<S>3816</S>.
KJV.10: For thy mercy<S>2617</S> is great<S>1419</S> unto the heavens<S>8064</S>, and thy truth<S>571</S> unto the clouds<S>7834</S>.
KJV.11: Be thou exalted<S>7311</S>, O God<S>430</S>, above the heavens<S>8064</S>: let thy glory<S>3519</S> be above all the earth<S>776</S>.

ESV.1: Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful to me, for in you my soul takes refuge; in the shadow of your wings I will take refuge, till the storms of destruction pass by.
ESV.2: I cry out to God Most High, to God who fulfills his purpose for me.
ESV.3: He will send from heaven and save me; he will put to shame him who tramples on me. Selah God will send out his steadfast love and his faithfulness!
ESV.4: My soul is in the midst of lions; I lie down amid fiery beasts — the children of man, whose teeth are spears and arrows, whose tongues are sharp swords.
ESV.5: Be exalted, O God, above the heavens! Let your glory be over all the earth!
ESV.6: They set a net for my steps; my soul was bowed down. They dug a pit in my way, but they have fallen into it themselves. Selah
ESV.7: My heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast! I will sing and make melody!
ESV.8: Awake, my glory!  Awake, O harp and lyre! I will awake the dawn!
ESV.9: I will give thanks to you, O Lord, among the peoples; I will sing praises to you among the nations.
ESV.10: For your steadfast love is great to the heavens, your faithfulness to the clouds.
ESV.11: Be exalted, O God, above the heavens! Let your glory be over all the earth!

NASB.1: For the choir director; [set to] Al-tashheth. A Mikhtam of David, when he fled from Saul in the cave. Be gracious to me, O God, be gracious to me, For my soul takes refuge in You; And in the shadow of Your wings I will take refuge Until destruction passes by.
NASB.2: I will cry to God Most High, To God who accomplishes [all things] for me.
NASB.3: He will send from heaven and save me; He reproaches him who tramples upon me. Selah. God will send forth His lovingkindness and His truth.
NASB.4: My soul is among lions; I must lie among those who breathe forth fire, [Even] the sons of men, whose teeth are spears and arrows And their tongue a sharp sword.
NASB.5: Be exalted above the heavens, O God; [Let] Your glory [be] above all the earth.
NASB.6: They have prepared a net for my steps; My soul is bowed down; They dug a pit before me; They [themselves] have fallen into the midst of it. Selah.
NASB.7: My heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast; I will sing, yes, I will sing praises!
NASB.8: Awake, my glory! Awake, harp and lyre! I will awaken the dawn.
NASB.9: I will give thanks to You, O Lord, among the peoples; I will sing praises to You among the nations.
NASB.10: For Your lovingkindness is great to the heavens And Your truth to the clouds.
NASB.11: Be exalted above the heavens, O God; [Let] Your glory [be] above all the earth.

NLT.1:
Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy!
I look to you for protection.
I will hide beneath the shadow of your wings
until the danger passes by.
NLT.2:
I cry out to God Most High,
to God who will fulfill his purpose for me.
NLT.3:
He will send help from heaven to rescue me,
disgracing those who hound me. Interlude
My God will send forth his unfailing love and faithfulness.
NLT.4:
I am surrounded by fierce lions
who greedily devour human prey —
whose teeth pierce like spears and arrows,
and whose tongues cut like swords.
NLT.5:
Be exalted, O God, above the highest heavens!
May your glory shine over all the earth.
NLT.6:
My enemies have set a trap for me.
I am weary from distress.
They have dug a deep pit in my path,
but they themselves have fallen into it. Interlude
NLT.7:
My heart is confident in you, O God;
my heart is confident.
No wonder I can sing your praises!
NLT.8:
Wake up, my heart!
Wake up, O lyre and harp!
I will wake the dawn with my song.
NLT.9:
I will thank you, Lord, among all the people.
I will sing your praises among the nations.
NLT.10:
For your unfailing love is as high as the heavens.
Your faithfulness reaches to the clouds.
NLT.11:
Be exalted, O God, above the highest heavens.
May your glory shine over all the earth.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” A poem by David, when he fled from Saul, in the cave. Be merciful to me, God, be merciful to me, for my soul takes refuge in you. Yes, in the shadow of your wings, I will take refuge, until disaster has passed.
WEB.2: I cry out to God Most High, to God who accomplishes my requests for me.
WEB.3: He will send from heaven, and save me, he rebukes the one who is pursuing me. Selah. God will send out his loving kindness and his truth.
WEB.4: My soul is among lions. I lie among those who are set on fire, even the sons of men, whose teeth are spears and arrows, and their tongue a sharp sword.
WEB.5: Be exalted, God, above the heavens! Let your glory be above all the earth!
WEB.6: They have prepared a net for my steps. My soul is bowed down. They dig a pit before me. They fall into the middle of it themselves. Selah.
WEB.7: My heart is steadfast, God, my heart is steadfast. I will sing, yes, I will sing praises.
WEB.8: Wake up, my glory! Wake up, lute and harp! I will wake up the dawn.
WEB.9: I will give thanks to you, Lord, among the peoples. I will sing praises to you among the nations.
WEB.10: For your great loving kindness reaches to the heavens, and your truth to the skies.
WEB.11: Be exalted, God, above the heavens. Let your glory be over all the earth.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: “Do Not Destroy.” A of David. When he fled before Saul into the cave. ⓛ [1] Be gracious to me, God, be gracious to me,
for I take refuge in you.
I will seek refuge in the shadow of your wings
until danger passes. ⓜ
CSB17.2:
I call to God Most High,
to God who fulfills his purpose for me. [1] ⓝ
CSB17.3:
He reaches down from heaven and saves me,
challenging the one who tramples me. ⓐ Selah God sends his faithful love and truth. ⓑ
CSB17.4:
I am surrounded by lions;
I lie down among devouring lions —
people whose teeth are spears and arrows,
whose tongues are sharp swords. ⓒ
CSB17.5:
God, be exalted above the heavens;
let your glory be over the whole earth. ⓓ
CSB17.6:
They prepared a net for my steps;
I was despondent.
They dug a pit ahead of me,
but they fell into it! ⓔ Selah
CSB17.7:
My heart is confident, God, my heart is confident.
I will sing; I will sing praises. ⓕ
CSB17.8:
Wake up, my soul!
Wake up, harp and lyre!
I will wake up the dawn. ⓖ
CSB17.9:
I will praise you, Lord, among the peoples;
I will sing praises to you among the nations. ⓗ
CSB17.10:
For your faithful love is as high as the heavens;
your faithfulness reaches the clouds. ⓘ
CSB17.11:
God, be exalted above the heavens; ⓙ
let your glory be over the whole earth. ⓚ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; set to [the tune of] "Do Not Destroy." A Mikhtam of David. [A record of memorable thoughts of David] when he fled from Saul in the cave. Be gracious to me, O God, be gracious and merciful to me,
For my soul finds shelter and safety in You,
And in the shadow of Your wings I will take refuge and be confidently secure
Until destruction passes by.
AMP.2:
I will cry to God Most High,
Who accomplishes all things on my behalf [for He completes my purpose in His plan].
AMP.3:
He will send from heaven and save me;
He calls to account him who tramples me down. Selah.
God will send out His lovingkindness and His truth.
AMP.4:
My life is among lions;
I must lie among those who breathe out fire—
The sons of men whose teeth are spears and arrows,
And their tongue a sharp sword.
AMP.5:
Be exalted above the heavens, O God;
Let Your glory and majesty be over all the earth.
AMP.6:
They set a net for my steps;
My very life was bowed down.
They dug a pit before me;
Into the midst of it they themselves have fallen. Selah.
AMP.7:
My heart is steadfast, O God, my heart is steadfast and confident!
I will sing, yes, I will sing praises [to You]!
AMP.8:
Awake, my glory!
Awake, harp and lyre!
I will awaken the dawn.
AMP.9:
I will praise and give thanks to You, O Lord, among the people;
I will sing praises to You among the nations.
AMP.10:
For Your faithfulness and lovingkindness are great, reaching to the heavens,
And Your truth to the clouds.
AMP.11:
Be exalted above the heavens, O God;
Let Your glory and majesty be over all the earth.

MSG.1:
A David psalm, when he hid in a cave from Saul.
Be good to me, God—and now!
I've run to you for dear life.
I'm hiding out under your wings
until the hurricane blows over.
MSG.2:
I call out to High God,
the God who holds me together.
MSG.3:
He sends orders from heaven and saves me,
he humiliates those who kick me around.
God delivers generous love,
he makes good on his word.
MSG.4:
I find myself in a pride of lions
who are wild for a taste of human flesh;
Their teeth are lances and arrows,
their tongues are sharp daggers.
MSG.5:
Soar high in the skies, O God!
Cover the whole earth with your glory!
MSG.6:
They booby trapped my path;
I thought I was dead and done for.
They dug a mantrap to catch me,
and fell in headlong themselves.
MSG.7:
I'm ready, God, so ready,
ready from head to toe,
Ready to sing, ready to raise a tune:
MSG.8:
"Wake up, soul!
Wake up, harp! wake up, lute!
Wake up, you sleepyhead sun!"
MSG.9:
I'm thanking you, God, out loud in the streets,
singing your praises in town and country.
MSG.10:
The deeper your love, the higher it goes;
every cloud is a flag to your faithfulness.
MSG.11:
Soar high in the skies, O God!
Cover the whole earth with your glory!

NIV.1:
Psalm 57
For the director of music. To the tune of “Do Not Destroy.” Of David. A miktam. When he had fled from Saul into the cave.
Have mercy on me, O God, have mercy on me,
for in you my soul takes refuge.
I will take refuge in the shadow of your wings
until the disaster has passed.
NIV.2:
I cry out to God Most High,
to God, who fulfills his purpose for me.
NIV.3:
He sends from heaven and saves me,
rebuking those who hotly pursue me; Selah
God sends his love and his faithfulness.
NIV.4:
I am in the midst of lions;
I lie among ravenous beasts—
men whose teeth are spears and arrows,
whose tongues are sharp swords.
NIV.5:
Be exalted, O God, above the heavens;
let your glory be over all the earth.
NIV.6:
They spread a net for my feet—
I was bowed down in distress.
They dug a pit in my path—
but they have fallen into it themselves. Selah
NIV.7:
My heart is steadfast, O God,
my heart is steadfast;
I will sing and make music.
NIV.8:
Awake, my soul!
Awake, harp and lyre!
I will awaken the dawn.
NIV.9:
I will praise you, O Lord, among the nations;
I will sing of you among the peoples.
NIV.10:
For great is your love, reaching to the heavens;
your faithfulness reaches to the skies.
NIV.11:
Be exalted, O God, above the heavens;
let your glory be over all the earth.

NRSVCE.1:
Be merciful to me, O God, be merciful to me,
for in you my soul takes refuge;
in the shadow of your wings I will take refuge,
until the destroying storms pass by.
NRSVCE.2:
I cry to God Most High,
to God who fulfills his purpose for me.
NRSVCE.3:
He will send from heaven and save me,
he will put to shame those who trample on me. Selah
God will send forth his steadfast love and his faithfulness.
NRSVCE.4:
I lie down among lions
that greedily devour human prey;
their teeth are spears and arrows,
their tongues sharp swords.
NRSVCE.5:
Be exalted, O God, above the heavens.
Let your glory be over all the earth.
NRSVCE.6:
They set a net for my steps;
my soul was bowed down.
They dug a pit in my path,
but they have fallen into it themselves. Selah
NRSVCE.7:
My heart is steadfast, O God,
my heart is steadfast.
I will sing and make melody.
NRSVCE.8:
Awake, my soul!
Awake, O harp and lyre!
I will awake the dawn.
NRSVCE.9:
I will give thanks to you, O Lord, among the peoples;
I will sing praises to you among the nations.
NRSVCE.10:
For your steadfast love is as high as the heavens;
your faithfulness extends to the clouds.
NRSVCE.11:
Be exalted, O God, above the heavens.
Let your glory be over all the earth.

NET.1: Have mercy on me, O God! Have mercy on me! For in you I have taken shelter. In the shadow of your wings I take shelter until trouble passes.
NET.2: I cry out for help to the sovereign God, to the God who vindicates me.
NET.3: May he send help from heaven and deliver me from my enemies who hurl insults! (Selah) May God send his loyal love and faithfulness!
NET.4: I am surrounded by lions; I lie down among those who want to devour me; men whose teeth are spears and arrows, whose tongues are a sharp sword.
NET.5: Rise up above the sky, O God! May your splendor cover the whole earth!
NET.6: They have prepared a net to trap me; I am discouraged. They have dug a pit for me. They will fall into it! (Selah)
NET.7: I am determined, O God! I am determined! I will sing and praise you!
NET.8: Awake, my soul! Awake, O stringed instrument and harp! I will wake up at dawn!
NET.9: I will give you thanks before the nations, O Master! I will sing praises to you before foreigners!
NET.10: For your loyal love extends beyond the sky, and your faithfulness reaches the clouds.
NET.11: Rise up above the sky, O God! May your splendor cover the whole earth!
